articles.title: Lakers Secure Home‑Court Advantage for Rockets First‑Round Series
articles.original_title: Schedule for Lakers vs. Rockets first round playoff series
articles.summary: Silver Screen and Roll reported that the Los Angeles Lakers will enter the upcoming playoffs with home‑court advantage for the second consecutive year, setting up a first‑round series against the Houston Rockets. The preview notes that the Lakers’ roster has changed significantly since their previous postseason meeting.
key_points: Lakers hold the higher seed and will host Games 1, 2, 5 and 7 if necessary.
The Rockets face a revamped Lakers lineup compared to their last playoff encounter.
Analysts remain cautious about the series outlook despite the home‑court edge.

Entry period and eligibility details are provided on the NBA website.

articles.title: Warriors vs. Clippers Play-In Preview
articles.original_title: Preview: Warriors take on Clippers in play-in matchup
articles.summary: The Warriors head into a do-or-die play-in game against the Clippers, with Stephen Curry and a partially healthy roster hoping to avoid another offseason. The winner advances, while the loser’s season ends.
key_points: Stephen Curry leads a limited Warriors lineup.
Game decides which team moves on to the playoffs.
Losers will face a long offseason.

articles.title: LaMelo Ball's Late Drive Powers Hornets Past Heat
articles.original_title: Ball's clutch drive saves Hornets in frantic finish
articles.summary: LaMelo Ball hit the go‑ahead basket with 4.7 seconds left in overtime, and Miles Bridges sealed the win with a buzzer‑beating block, eliminating the Miami Heat from the play‑in tournament.
key_points: Ball's clutch shot gave Charlotte a one‑point lead.
Bridges' block on the final possession secured the victory.
The Hornets advance, while the Heat are eliminated.

articles.title: Bam Adebayo Injured, Leaves Heat Game
articles.original_title: Spo: Ball should've been tossed for Adebayo trip
articles.summary: Miami’s center Bam Adebayo suffered a lower‑back injury after a fall in the second quarter and was ruled out for the remainder of the play‑in contest.
key_points: Injury occurred in the second quarter.
Adebayo was ruled out for the second half.
Heat had to adjust without their starting big man.

articles.title: Avdija Scores 41 as Blazers Clinch No. 7 Seed
articles.original_title: Blazers ride Avdija's 41 over Suns, grab 7th seed
articles.summary: Deni Avdija poured in 41 points, leading Portland to erase an 11‑point fourth‑quarter deficit and beat the Phoenix Suns 114‑110, securing the Western Conference’s seventh seed.
key_points: Avdija’s 41 points set a franchise‑playoff‑seed record.
Blazers rallied from an 11‑point hole in the fourth quarter.
Win locked the 7th seed and a first‑round matchup.

articles.title: Amazon Stream Outage During Heat‑Hornets Play‑In Overtime
articles.original_title: Amazon feed cuts out late in Heat-Hornets thriller
articles.summary: During the overtime period of the Miami‑Charlotte play‑in game, Amazon Prime Video’s live feed went dark for nearly two minutes, causing viewers to miss a Hornets possession.
key_points: Technical outage lasted about two minutes.
The interruption occurred in overtime.
Fans missed a critical Hornets offensive play.

articles.original_title: J. Cole ends stint with Chinese pro hoops team
articles.summary: Rap megastar J. Cole said Tuesday that his time playing professional basketball in China has come to an end after one game.

articles.title: Bronny's Nike 'B9' logo trademark filing denied
articles.original_title: Bronny's Nike 'B9' logo trademark filing denied
articles.summary: Nike's trademark filing for Lakers guard Bronny James' "B9" logo has been refused because of a "likelihood of confusion" with an already-registered mark.

articles.title: No update on Doncic as Lakers prep for Rockets
articles.original_title: No update on Doncic as Lakers prep for Rockets
articles.summary: After putting his team through its first practice in preparation for the playoffs, Los Angeles Lakers coach JJ Redick said Tuesday he doesn't have any more information about when Luka Doncic could return, only saying that the star is "out indefinitely."

articles.title: Lakers Get Major Playoff Boost Amid Luka Doncic, Austin Reaves Injuries
articles.original_title: Lakers Get Major Playoff Boost Amid Luka Doncic, Austin Reaves Injuries - heavy.com
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers receive a significant advantage in the playoff race after Dallas Mavericks star Luka Doncic and Lakers guard Austin Reaves each suffer injuries. Doncic’s absence weakens a direct competitor, while Reaves’ injury forces the Lakers to adjust their rotation heading into the postseason.
key_points: Luka Doncic is sidelined with a sprained ankle, reducing the Mavericks' playoff threat.
Austin Reaves suffers a minor knee strain, limiting his minutes for the Lakers.
The injuries shift the Western Conference landscape, improving the Lakers' odds of advancing.

articles.title: LeBron James' Next Career Move: New Intel from His Camp
articles.original_title: Huge update on LeBron James' next career stop: LeBron camp's intel revealed - BasketNews.com
articles.summary: BasketNews.com reports that sources close to LeBron James' camp have disclosed potential scenarios for his next team after his current contract expires. The information suggests the NBA star is weighing options that include staying in Los Angeles, joining a contender, or pursuing a player‑development role.
key_points: LeBron’s camp is evaluating both on‑court and off‑court opportunities for the 2026‑27 season.
Staying with the Lakers remains a possibility, but interest from other franchises has increased.
No final decision has been made; the situation remains fluid.

articles.title: Channing Frye Says LeBron Focused on Winning, Not Age, in Playoffs
articles.original_title: Channing Frye Says LeBron James Focused on Winning, Not Age, In Playoff Series - TMZ
articles.summary: Former NBA player Channing Frye told TMZ that LeBron James approaches the current playoff series with a win‑first mindset, emphasizing performance over concerns about his age. Frye highlighted James’ preparation and leadership as key factors for the Lakers’ postseason push.
key_points: LeBron is concentrating on team success rather than personal milestones.
Frye praised James’ conditioning and game‑film study habits.
The veteran’s attitude is influencing younger Lakers players positively.

articles.title: LeBron James Admits He Doesn’t Know How Many Postseasons Remain
articles.original_title: "I don't know how many more postseasons I got left in my career" - LeBron James says he's cherishing every playoff moment late in his career - Basketball Network
articles.summary: In an interview with Basketball Network, LeBron James reflected on his longevity, stating he is uncertain how many more playoff appearances he has left but is savoring each moment. He emphasized the importance of competing at a high level and enjoying the experience with his teammates.
key_points: James acknowledges the uncertainty of his remaining playoff years.
He expressed gratitude for the opportunity to compete at an elite level.
The comments underline his continued commitment to the Lakers’ postseason goals.

articles.title: Stein: Growing Anticipation That LeBron James Could Leave Lakers
articles.original_title: Stein: There's 'growing anticipation' LeBron James could leave Lakers - LeBron Wire
articles.summary: LeBron Wire cites NBA analyst Rich Stein, who notes increasing speculation that LeBron James may depart the Los Angeles Lakers after his contract ends. Stein points to roster construction, salary‑cap considerations, and the player’s desire for a championship as factors fueling the chatter.
key_points: Stein observes heightened rumors about a possible Lakers exit for LeBron.
Cap space and roster flexibility are cited as primary logistical concerns.
No official indication from James or the organization confirms a move.
